concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a meaningful agency logo ('NC Waste Management Environmental Quality') at the top of page 1 and a signature next to the 'From' line. The HTML contains corresponding tags, but according to the provided image manifest, all images have 'valid: false' due to an extraction error ('No module named PIL'). Because the base64 data is corrupt/truncated, these images will be broken and fail to render in the browser.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains the NC DEQ 'Waste Management ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' logo at the top left and a handwritten signature next to 'Shawn McKee'.
concerns.output_quote: The image manifest indicates 'valid': false for all images on page 1 (including the logo and signature), meaning the base64 image data is corrupt and will result in broken images.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Mana; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification; Meeting Officer’s Report
Coal Ash Impoundment Classification
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

The source document makes extensive use of underlined text to introduce bulleted items on pages 3, 4, and 5 (e.g., 'Dam Safety', 'Excavation', 'Groundwater Assessments'). In the JSON output, these list items are extracted as plain text without any markdown or indication of structural emphasis (bold/underline) being preserved.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Dam Safety – There was a concern... (where 'Dam Safety' is bolded and underlined in the PDF)
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Dam Safety – There was a concern of a huge impact on the Lumber River..."

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (2 list(s)): p1:letter+roman, p5:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=2. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.
